title: Red Sox place Trevor Story on injured list with sports hernia, contemplating surgery
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox placed Trevor Story on the 10-day injured list with a sports hernia, considering possible surgery.
Nick Sogard was recalled from Triple-A Worcester as infielder Trevor Story's replacement on the roster.
Story has a .206 batting average and .958 fielding percentage, committing six errors in 39 games at shortstop.

title: One driver dead in NH collision between pickup truck and school bus
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A 74-year-old man was killed in a head-on collision with a school bus in Marlborough, New Hampshire.
Thirteen students aboard the bus were not seriously injured; they were taken to hospitals as a precaution.
The crash on Route 12 is under investigation by New Hampshire State Police and local authorities.

title: Carl Yastrzemski’s grandson, Braves’ Mike Yastrzemski, walks off Red Sox
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Atlanta Braves' Mike Yastrzemski hit a walk-off single in the 10th inning to beat the Boston Red Sox 3-2.
Braves pitcher Connelly Early allowed two solo home runs over five innings, striking out six without issuing a walk.
The Red Sox left seven players on base, going 1-for-3 with runners in scoring position on seven hits.

title: Report: Reunion between Patriots, Stefon Diggs viewed as unlikely despite mutual interest
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Stefon Diggs interested in returning to Patriots, but trade for A.J. Brown complicates potential reunion.
Patriots will assume $32 million AAV for Brown, adding financial constraints on re-signing Diggs.
Albert Breer suggests Diggs unlikely to return due to cap space issues, despite team's interest.

title: Boston police seek missing boy, 15
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: 15-year-old Lafayette LaGrant-Gomes was last seen on Tuesday near 965 Blue Hill Ave in Dorchester, according to Boston police.
Boston Police are actively investigating and ask for public help to locate LaGrant-Gomes, known to visit multiple local areas.
Contact Boston Police District B-3 or use CrimeStoppers for any information regarding LaGrant-Gomes' disappearance.

title: Police: Teen allegedly shot girlfriend’s mother in the head on Mother’s Day
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Tyler Broomes, 18, accused of shooting his girlfriend's mother and another man on Mother's Day in Dorchester.
The shooting occurred outside a Kingsdale Street home during an argument; both victims, aged 42, survived.
Broomes charged with assault with intent to murder; held without bail pending hearing on May 21.

title: How would a team made up entirely of former Red Sox look in 2026?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Former Red Sox players, including Mookie Betts and Xander Bogaerts, continue to perform well for other teams in 2026.
Jared Carrabis highlighted that five former Red Sox pitchers have a combined 2.74 ERA this season.
Boston Red Sox's roster decisions are questioned as former players thrive, raising hypothetical competitiveness of an all-former-Red Sox team.

title: Red Sox’ Roman Anthony no longer wearing wrist brace, played catch Friday
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Roman Anthony of the Red Sox was placed on the 10-day injured list on May 7 due to a right wrist sprain.
Anthony has removed his wrist brace and played catch, but has not resumed swinging a bat yet.
Interim manager Chad Tracy is hopeful Anthony can start batting soon, possibly by the end of the Atlanta series.

title: Jeep drives into Norwood day care
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: A Jeep accidentally crashed into BrightPath daycare in Norwood, causing moderate damage but no injuries.
The driver was dropping off a passenger and mistakenly accelerated into the building, according to Norwood police.
The daycare was temporarily shut down for structural assessment following an inspection by the town building inspector.

title: Here’s what experts are predicting the Patriots’ 2026 record will be
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Experts predict a 10-7 regular-season record for the Patriots in 2026, down from 14-3 last season.
Patriots face a challenging 2026 schedule with top opponents like Seattle, Denver Broncos, and Chicago Bears.
While improvements were made, experts cite tougher schedule as reason for fewer wins compared to 2025.
